claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! I'm here to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:

**How old is your child?**

This is important because eligibility rules differ based on age:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Young people **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Those **aged 20 or over** typically don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know their ages too.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex is 8 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Let me continue with the assessment for Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's great. Now I need to check a few more conditions:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

At 8 years old, Alex almost certainly isn't working, but I need to confirm this as part of the standard assessment process.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 8, so he's just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you for confirming that. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now the next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in Alex's own name (not yours as the parent). 

For an 8-year-old at school, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - is Alex receiving any benefits like ESA or Universal Credit in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name - he's just a regular 8-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

Again, for an 8-year-old this would be extremely unusual (apprenticeships are typically for 16+), but I need to confirm as part of the complete assessment. Is Alex currently enrolled in any apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's only 8 and just goes to primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to check about your residency status. To qualify for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

Or if not, do any of these apply to you:
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
- You're living abroad as a Crown servant (e.g., working for the UK government overseas)
- Other special circumstances related to EU Settlement Scheme status

The simplest path is if you normally live in the UK. Do you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Since you live in Manchester, you meet the UK residency requirement. Now the next check:

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You mentioned earlier that Alex lives at home with you, which means you do meet this requirement. Responsibility is established by either:
- Living with the child (which you do), OR
- Contributing to their upkeep (at least as much as the Child Benefit amount)

Since Alex lives with you, you clearly meet this requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- Anyone else

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. If someone else is claiming, they would need to agree to stop their claim for you to take it over.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check about Alex's living situation:

**Which of the following best describes Alex's current situation?**

- Living normally at home with you (not in hospital, care, or foster care)
- In hospital
- In local authority care
- In foster care

You mentioned Alex lives at home with you, so I assume it's the first option, but please confirm which applies to Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is living normally at home with me. He's not in hospital, care, or foster care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for Alex's living costs or upkeep? Generally, if the local authority is paying for a child's accommodation or maintenance, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

Since Alex lives at home with you in Manchester, I assume the council is not paying for his accommodation or maintenance, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. I'm looking after him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Let me record the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE** ✓

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why You're Eligible:
- ✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- ✓ Alex is not working
- ✓ Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✓ Alex is not in an apprenticeship
- ✓ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✓ You have responsibility for Alex (living together)
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✓ Alex lives at home with you (not in hospital, care, or foster care)
- ✓ The local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply (this is a tax charge, but you can still claim)
5. **Payment schedule:** Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

You can find more information and apply here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives at home in the UK with the claimant who is responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all UK Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 8-year-old child Alex. The claimant is UK resident (living in Manchester), has responsibility for Alex who lives with them, and no other factors disqualify the claim. The claimant should proceed to apply for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, and lives at home in the UK with the claimant who is responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all UK Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 8-year-old child Alex. The claimant is UK resident (living in Manchester), has responsibility for Alex who lives with them, and no other factors disqualify the claim. The claimant should proceed to apply for Child Benefit.
